name: disposing a value-less owns edge is swallowed by the idempotency refresh
description: NEW GAP (distinct from the 4 known). A model that emits owns as a bare object edge (no value_json, object_entity_ref=the thing) cannot end ownership. The sell note re-uses the SAME object entity with assertion=negated. supersession.values_equal returns True on object_entity_id alone (value_json is None on both sides, so it falls to 'object_entity_id is not None -> True'), so decide() takes the re-extraction idempotency branch and returns refresh_id. pipeline._upsert_fact's refresh path (pipeline.py ~lines 549-564) updates only statement/extractor/prompt_version/confidence -- it never writes the new assertion or closes the interval. Result: ONE row, status=active, assertion=asserted, with the 'no longer own' statement -- ownership wrongly persists as actively asserted. Spec-correct (docs/ANALYSIS.md 'Assertion status': the wiki demotes everything below asserted; a negated owns is not an owned fact): the head must be negated/closed. xfail until values_equal or the refresh path accounts for an assertion flip.
xfail: owns disposal lost: values_equal matches on object_entity_id alone so a negated re-statement hits the refresh_id idempotency path (supersession.values_equal -> pipeline._upsert_fact refresh branch), which never updates assertion

facts.statement: I no longer own the Civic.
